tools.name: get_project_context
tools.description:
Captures the user's project architecture to inform i18n implementation strategy.

## When to Use

**Called during i18n_checklist Step 1.**

The checklist tool will tell you when to call this. If you're implementing i18n:
1. Call i18n_checklist(step_number=1, done=false) FIRST
2. The checklist will i

tools.name: get_framework_docs
tools.description:
Retrieves authoritative documentation directly from the framework's official repository.

## When to Use

**Called during i18n_checklist Steps 1-13.**

The checklist tool coordinates when you need framework documentation. Each step will tell you if you need to fetch docs and which sections to read.

tools.name: get_i18n_library_docs
tools.description:
Retrieves authoritative documentation for i18n libraries (currently react-intl).

## When to Use

**Called during i18n_checklist Steps 7-10.**

The checklist tool will tell you when you need i18n library documentation. Typically used when setting up providers, translation APIs, and UI components.

tools.name: i18n_checklist
tools.description:
## ⚠️ MANDATORY TOOL FOR ALL I18N WORK ⚠️

THIS IS NOT OPTIONAL. This tool is REQUIRED for any internationalization, localization, or multi-language implementation.

## When to Use (MANDATORY)

**ALWAYS use this tool when the user says ANY of these phrases:**

- "set up i18n"
- "add internationaliza

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "get_project_context" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Captures the user's project architecture to inform i18n implementation strategy. ## When to Use **Called during i18n_checklist Step 1.** The checklist tool will…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "i18n_checklist" stacks urgency/override words (immediately, always, mandatory). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: ## ⚠️ MANDATORY TOOL FOR ALL I18N WORK ⚠️ THIS IS NOT OPTIONAL. This tool is REQUIRED for any internationalization, localization, or multi-language implementati…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "main" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: i18n_checklist · Private data: get_project_context, i18n_checklist · Public sink: i18n_checklist · Destructive: get_framework_docs, i18n_checklist.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: i18n_checklist · Private data: get_project_context, i18n_checklist · Public sink: i18n_checklist · Destructive: get_framework_docs, i18n_checklist

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
